text:
Market today: Mixed. BlackRock's strong start shows institutional confidence, but Buffett warns of 'gambling' over value, a direct hit to your tech/chip focus. Fed testimony is the immediate driver for growth names.

Your holdings: SOXL (-36.54%) and SPAL (-37.06%) are leveraged ETFs with significant losses and stops now above current prices. PTNM (-15.25%) is also down. Margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Monitor Fed Chairman Warsh's testimony for rate signals. Re-evaluate your exit plans for SOXL and SPAL given their deep losses and leveraged nature.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing your leveraged losers and stay cautious until the Fed's stance is clearer.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at 200 is now above the current price of 161.5. Given the "SELL" status and -36.54% loss, re-evaluate your exit strategy to manage this leveraged position.
SPAL: The stop at 21.38 is above its current price of 15.83. With a -37.06% loss and "NEW" status, consider adjusting your exit plan for this volatile, leveraged ETF.
No new buys today - hold and watch.
